The attack is so fast that it can't be seen as more than a blur unless the target has High Speed Sight 1 or higher, has an active Speed Rank of 1 or higher, or succeeds at a Spot check opposed by the attack roll.

If you are not wearing clothing with pockets near the waist, you suffer a -4 penalty to the Perform Requirements.

Mastery: The fifth step allows the technique to extend to a range 5 ft beyond your reach, provided there are no obstacles between you and the target, and the target does not benefit from any amount of cover.

name: KENSOKU: IAISOUKEN (SWIFT FISTS: TWIN UNDODGEABLE FISTS)

description.value:
Type: Taijutsu (Stance)

Rank: 7 (Spell Level 4)

Chakra Cost: 8

Learn DC: 21 | Successes: 3

Perform: 9 | Perform DC: 22

Components: Mob

All your unarmed attacks count as Kensoku: Iaiken attacks, with the same bonuses. You take a -2 penalty to attack rolls, and gain a +2 bonus to any rolls made to confirm critical hits with such attacks. Inflicting the dazed condition multiple times in a round does not extend its duration.

name: KENSOKU: OUGI - SHUNSHIN RENDAN (SWIFT FISTS: SECRET SKILL - BODY FLICKER COMBO)

description.value:
Type: Taijutsu (Strike)

Rank: 9 (Spell Level 5)

Chakra Cost: 16

Learn DC: 25 | Successes: 5

Perform: 13 | Perform DC: 29

Components: C, H

You seem to flicker in and out of existence as you make lightning quick strikes against many foes. Make an unarmed or melee weapon attack against the primary target, which deals 8d6 damage of a type according to the weapon used. Then, you leap to a secondary target within 20 ft to make an attack, then to another, attacking up to 1 secondary target per 2 levels, each within 20 ft of the previous one.

The secondary targets take half damage, and the attacks cannot score critical hits, nor can you target the same creature with more than one attack. The movement is made as though using Shunpo, leaving footprints and possibly springing traps.

Mastery: The first step allows you to deal nonlethal damage by taking a -4 penalty to the Perform check and the attack rolls, assuming your weapon allows for nonlethal attacks.

name: KIRIKAESU NO WAZA (METHOD OF COUNTER ATTACK)

description.value:
Type: Taijutsu (Stance)

Rank: 6 (Spell Level 3)

Chakra Cost: 0

Learn DC: 20 | Successes: 3

Perform: 8 | Perform DC: 21

Components: None

While in this stance, each time an adjacent opponent misses you with a melee attack, you may make an at- tack of opportunity as an instant action costing 6 Chakra. You gain a +2 bonus to the attack roll for such attacks.

Type: Taijutsu (Stance)

Rank: 3 (Spell Level 2)

Chakra Cost: 2

Learn DC: 16 | Successes: 2

Perform: 4 | Perform DC: 16

Components: F, Mas, Mob

You enter a special fighting stance that allows you to make expert use of a battle wire as a weapon. If you are wearing battle wire(s), they can be 'drawn' (released from the gauntlet) as a free action, and can be retracted as a move action that does not provoke attacks of opportunity. Alternatively, you can use two lengths or iron wire, but they must be drawn or stowed normally, and you suffer a -4 penalty to meet the Perform Requirements, as well as taking the same penalty to attack rolls when using the iron wire to attack.

Though wire weapons are considered ranged weapons, they are not classified as either projectile or thrown weapons, and so do not receive any bonuses specific to such weapons (such as Shuriken Expert abilities). Abilities that apply to all ranged weapons do affect wire weapons, with the exception of the Far Shot feat. While fighting with wires, you add your Dexterity modifier to damage rolls.

When you threaten a critical hit with a wire attack, you can add up to three Sneak Attack dice to the damage (if you have them), even if you do not meet the usual requirements for doing so (Targets immune to sneak attacks are still immune to this extra damage).

If you roll a natural 1 on an attack roll with a wire, you have a 50% chance to suffer 1d4 damage as the wires slash you.

Mastery: The third step allows you to make attacks with wire weapons without provoking attacks of opportunity.

name: KOUSEN RYU: TETSURYUUHA (IRON WIRE STYLE: STEEL DRAGON BLAST)

description.value:
Type: Taijutsu (Strike)

Rank: 9 (Spell Level 5)

Chakra Cost: 2

Learn DC: 23 | Successes: 3

Perform: 11 | Perform DC: 24

Components: F, Mob

You must be in the Kousen Ryu stance to use this technique, which allows you to make a single attack with your wire weapon against all creatures in the area.

Such attacks deal normal damage but cannot score a critical hit. The range is 20 ft when using iron wire, or 30 ft when using battle wire.

Empower: Spend 1 Chakra to increase the base damage of the wire weapon by 1 die (up to 5 extra dice). Can only be done if you have 2 or more Livewire levels.

Type: Taijutsu (Strike)

Rank: 4 (Spell Level 2)

Chakra Cost: 1

Learn DC: 17 | Successes: 2

Perform: 5 | Perform DC: 17

Components: F, Mob

You draw the string of your greatbow to it's fullest potential and loose a powerful shot. Make an attack against the target. If the target is within 5 range increments, you can apply the Power Attack feat (despite it not being a melee attack) to the attack.

You may choose to increase the perform time to a full-round action to gain a +2 bonus to the attack roll.

name: KYUJUTSU: KESSEN OUGI - SOUGEKI (BOW ART: ABSOLUTE FIGHTING SKILL - SNIPING SHOT)

description.value:
Type: Taijutsu (Strike)

Rank: 9 (Spell Level 5)

Chakra Cost: 10

Learn DC: 24 | Successes: 4

Perform: 12 | Perform DC: 26

Components: F, Mas Mob

Make a single attack against the target. If the attack hits, it automatically becomes a critical hit. If the target is immune to critical hits, the attack instead deals one additional die of damage (based on the weapon's damage die). A Samurai with at least 10 class levels may substitute this technique for a normal attack when they make a Full Attack (using the same attack bonus as the substituted attack), but only if they chose the Kyujutsu path, and only once per round. If you substitute the technique this way, the Perform Requirements are automatically met.

This technique can be used once per day.

Empower: Spend 1 Chakra to increase the bonus damage by 1d6, up to 1d6 per 2 levels or 6d6 total.

Mastery: The second and fourth steps each allow you to use this technique an additional time per day.

Type: Taijutsu (Strike)

Rank: 2 (Spell Level 1)

Chakra Cost: 2

Learn DC: 14 | Successes: 1

Perform: 2 | Perform DC: 13

Components: E, F, Mob

You make a single attack with the weapon, gaining a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

Type: Taijutsu (Strike)

Rank: 6 (Spell Level 3)

Chakra Cost: 3

Learn DC: 20 | Successes: 3

Perform: 8 | Perform DC: 21

Components: E, F, Mob

Make a single attack with your held projectile weapon. If the attack hits, it deals 1d6 bonus piercing damage. A Samurai with at least 6 class levels may substitute this technique for a normal attack when they make a Full Attack (using the same attack bonus as the substituted attack), but only if they chose the Kyujutsu path. If you substitute the technique this way, it cannot be empowered and the Perform Requirements are automatically met.

To use this technique, the target must be of a size category no larger than you, and must have made an armed melee attack against you since your last turn. You must also have both hands free. Initiate a grapple attempt against the target, which does not provoke an attack of opportunity. If successful, you throw the target into an adjacent square and knock it prone, and can make a free disarm check against the target with a +4 bonus. This check does not provoke an attack of opportunity.

Mastery: The first step allows you to use this technique even if the target has not recently attacked you.

The second step allows you to increase the perform time to a full-round action, allowing you to deal your normal unarmed attack damage on a successful grapple attempt.

The fifth step allows you to use the technique with only a single hand free, and you can throw the target up to 10 feet away (it is still left prone). If it collides with a solid object, it takes 1d6 fall damage, as though they had fallen 10 feet.

Type: Taijutsu (Strike)

To use this technique, the target must be of a size category no larger than you, and must have made an armed melee attack against you since your last turn. You must also have both hands free. Initiate a grapple attempt against the target, which does not provoke an attack of opportunity. If successful, you may immediately make a second grapple check to pin the target.

Mastery: The first step allows you to use this technique even if the target has not recently attacked you.

The third step allows you to use the technique with only a single hand free.

The fourth step allows you to reduce to perform time to an attack action once per day.

The fifth step allows you to use this technique against creatures up to one size category larger than you.

Type: Taijutsu (Strike)

Rank: 3 (Spell Level 2)

Learn DC: 15 | Successes: 1

To use this technique, the target must be of a size category no larger than you, and must have missed with an armed melee attack against you since your last turn. You must also have both hands free. Initiate a grapple attempt against the target, which does not provoke an attack of opportunity. If successful, you throw the target 10 ft in any direction, other than directly behind you. The creature is also knocked prone upon landing (Reflex negates).

If it collides with a solid object, it takes 1d6 fall damage, as though they had fallen 10 feet.

Mastery: The second and fourth steps each increase the distance you can throw the target by 5 ft, which also increases the effective fall damage by 1d6 / 10 ft per step.

The second step also allows you to use this technique as a full-round action. If you do, you deal normal unarmed attack damage on a successful grapple attempt.

The fifth step allows you to use this technique against a target that did not attack or miss you recently, and requires you only have a single hand free.

Make a single attack with a held weapon, which gains Kawarimi Defense 1 and cannot score a critical hit. The firs time this technique is used against a target in a 24 hour period, they are considered flat-footed against the attack, but you cannot apply more sneak attack dice than your Intelligence modifier +1 (maximum 5).

If the target was already flat-footed, this does not count as a 24-hourly use (for the purpose of the above), and there is no limit to how many sneak attack dice you may apply. A successful Hide check makes it appear as though you hadn't moved at all (Spot check negates).

You do not provoke attacks of opportunity for moving, and gain a +4 bonus to Defense and saves against any traps you might trigger while moving.

Avoiding an Attack (1/day): Minimum perform DC 17. If you succeed, you may move up to 15 ft, as above (without the 10 ft rule). Using the technique this way costs 2 Chakra. You can use this technique to avoid an attack against a source with a CR of up to 1.

Mastery: Each step allows you to use the technique to avoid an attack an additional time per day. Each step also increases the CR which you can avoid, at an increased Chakra cost.

The first step: CR 5.

The second step: Cost 3, CR 10. The third step: Cost 4, CR 15. The fourth step Cost 5, CR 20. The fifth step: Cost 6, CR 25.

Type: Taijutsu (Stance)

Rank: 9 (Spell Level 5)

Chakra Cost: 2

Learn DC: 23 | Successes: 3

Perform: 11 | Perform DC: 24

Components: Mas, Mob

While in this stance, you are able to travel great distances, calculated in leaps. Converting a move action into leaps costs 1 Chakra and grants 1 leap per 5 ft of movement speed you have. Non-permanent movement speed increases do not grant leaps.

Converting a round of hustling into leaps costs 3

Chakra, and grants the same number of leaps, plus your level, active Speed Rank and Dexterity modifier in leaps. You can convert one round of hustling per point of Constitution modifier per minute without penalty. Each time you convert two consecutive turns of hustling into leaps, and each round you convert a hustle into leaps beyond your limit per minute, you must make a Constitution check (DC 10, +1 per successful check you've made that day) to avoid becoming fatigued for 1 minute.

A leap will instantly move you 20 ft in any direction, halved to 10 ft on an uphill movement (up to a 45 degree incline) or increased to 30 ft on a downhill movement (up to a 45 degree decline). While wearing medium armor, the distance of leaps are halved. You cannot take any leaps while wearing heavy armor. While moving, you can only be seen by creatures succeeding at a Spot check opposed by your Hide check. You gain a +20 bonus to this check, and suffer no penalty for hiding while moving.

Beginning a leap in a threatened area may provoke attacks of opportunity, but moving through a threatened area while leaping does not. Each leap must begin on a solid surface that can support your weight, and if a leap does not end on such a surface you cannot take any further leaps.

You cannot leap through obstructed areas or areas that would decrease your movement speed. You still leave footprints and can still trigger traps, though you gain a +4 bonus to Defense and saves against such traps.

You cannot leap while unable to run or charge, encumbered, entangled, fatigued, exhausted or staggered.

Mastery: The first step allows you to halve the distance of your leaps when converting a move action to leaps.

The third step allows you to take leaps while encumbered, though not while heavily encumbered.

The fifth step allows you to convert a single move action into leaps at no cost if you do not convert any other actions to leaps that round.

On hit, the attack deals your normal unarmed attack damage, plus 2d6 damage of piercing, sonic and fire damage (each, 6d6 total). A successful save halves the bonus damage. Chakra Resistance only resists the bonus damage, not the normal unarmed attack damage.

If hit, the target loses its next turn. In addition, it loses its Dexterity bonus to Defense for the next attack made against it. Your next attack or technique must have the target as its (primary) target. If that technique is a

Taijutsu technique, you automatically succeed at the Perform check. If the subject dies or you become unable to reach it with your next attack or technique, you lose your next turn. If the follow-up attack is made in the same round as this technique, the target does not lose its next turn. You are considered flat-footed until your next action.

A target cannot be affected by this technique more than once every 3 rounds.

The target must be prone for this technique to work. Make a melee attack against the target, which deals damage as though you were one size category larger. You gain a +2 bonus to rolls to confirm a critical hit for this attack. This technique can be used once per encounter.

Mastery: The second and fourth step each allow you to use this technique an additional time per encounter.

name: TSUIJUTSU: KESSEN OUGI - METSUGEKI (HAMMER ART: ABSOLUTE FIGHTING SKILL - DOOMHAMMER)

description.value:

Make a single attack against the target, which gains a +4 bonus to its critical threat range. If the attack hits, it deals normal damage which ignores all hardness and

DR, and knocks the target back a number of feet equal to the damage dealt. If the target collides with a solid object during its knockback, it suffers damage as though it had fallen 20 feet (2d6).

A Samurai with at least 10 class levels may substitute this technique for a normal attack when they make a Full Attack (using the same attack bonus as the substituted attack), but only if they chose the Tsuijutsu path, and only once per round. If you substitute the technique this way, the Perform Requirements are automatically met.

This technique can be used once per day.

Mastery: The second and fourth steps each allow you to use this technique an additional time per day.

Make a single attack with your held weapon. If the attack hits, it deals normal damage, and the target is stunned for 1 round (Fortitude negates). A Samurai with at least 2 class levels may substitute this technique for a normal attack when they make a Full Attack (using the same attack bonus as the substituted attack), but only if they chose the Soujutsu path. If you substitute the technique this way, it cannot be empowered and the Perform Requirements are automatically met.

Empower: Spend 1 Chakra to increase the save DC by 1, up to +1 per 2 levels or +5.

You turn on the spot, revolving to create a whirling, dangerous dance. You may move up to your full movement speed, gaining a +4 deflection bonus to Defense against any attacks of opportunity made against you. As you move, you can make a single attack at each creature you pass, up to one creature per two levels (maximum 5), though you must move at least 5 ft between attacks. Creatures affected must make a Spot check (DC 10) to avoid losing their Dexterity bonus to Defense against the attack, though you cannot apply sneak attack damage to the attacks.

You become nauseated for 1 round after completing this technique (Fortitude, DC 11 + 2 per attack made negates).

This stance will overlap with any bonuses granted by other stances, and can be used at the same time. This stance can be used only once per encounter, and automatically ends if you leave total defense for any reason.

Mastery: The second and fourth steps each allow this technique to be used an additional time per encounter.
